title: Gut microbiome alpha-synuclein aggregation signatures in elderly Parkinson's disease patients
abstract: We profiled the fecal microbiome of 300 older adults with Parkinson's disease and matched controls and related community composition to enteric alpha-synuclein pathology and motor scores. Depletion of Prevotellaceae and enrichment of Akkermansia tracked disease severity, suggesting a gut-brain axis contribution to neurodegeneration. Although this is gut-microbiome research, it concerns adult neurodegenerative disease rather than infant growth, childhood undernutrition, or complementary-food intervention.
